mcqs.question: What is the main function in C++?

options.text: The entry point of any C++ program
A user-defined function
A system-defined function
A macro

mcqs.explanation: The main function is the entry point of any C++ program.

mcqs.question: What are header files used for in C++?

options.text: To declare functions, variables, and classes that can be used in multiple source files
To define functions, variables, and classes that can be used in multiple source files
To include the contents of a source file into a header file
To exclude the contents of a header file from a source file

mcqs.explanation: Header files are used to declare functions, variables, and classes that can be used in multiple source files.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the #include directive in C++?

options.text: To include the contents of a header file into a source file
To exclude the contents of a header file from a source file
To declare a function, variable, or class
To define a function, variable, or class

mcqs.explanation: The #include directive is used to include the contents of a header file into a source file.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between #include <filename> and #include "filename"?

options.text: The first form is used for system header files, while the second form is used for user-defined header files
The first form is used for user-defined header files, while the second form is used for system header files
The first form is used for header files in the current directory, while the second form is used for header files in other directories
The first form is used for header files in other directories, while the second form is used for header files in the current directory

mcqs.explanation: The first form is used for system header files, while the second form is used for user-defined header files.

mcqs.question: What is the main function signature in C++?

options.text: int main()
void main()
int main(int argc, char* argv[])
void main(int argc, char* argv[])

mcqs.explanation: The main function signature is int main() or int main(int argc, char* argv[]).

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the return statement in the main function?

options.text: To indicate the program's exit status
To return a value to the caller
To terminate the program
To restart the program

mcqs.explanation: The return statement is used to indicate the program's exit status.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the argc and argv parameters in the main function?

options.text: To represent the number of command-line arguments and an array of strings containing the command-line arguments
To represent the number of command-line arguments and a single string containing the command-line arguments
To represent a single command-line argument and an array of strings containing the command-line arguments
To represent a single command-line argument and a single string containing the command-line arguments

mcqs.explanation: The argc parameter represents the number of command-line arguments, while the argv parameter represents an array of strings containing the command-line arguments.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the #ifndef directive in C++?

options.text: To prevent multiple inclusions of a header file
To allow multiple inclusions of a header file
To declare a function, variable, or class
To define a function, variable, or class

mcqs.explanation: The #ifndef directive is used to prevent multiple inclusions of a header file.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the #define directive in C++?

options.text: To define a macro
To declare a function, variable, or class
To include the contents of a header file into a source file
To exclude the contents of a header file from a source file

mcqs.explanation: The #define directive is used to define a macro.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the #endif directive in C++?

options.text: To end a conditional compilation block
To begin a conditional compilation block
To declare a function, variable, or class
To define a function, variable, or class

mcqs.explanation: The #endif directive is used to end a conditional compilation block.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the using namespace std; directive in C++?

options.text: To bring the standard library namespace into scope
To declare a function, variable, or class
To define a function, variable, or class
To exclude the standard library namespace from scope

mcqs.explanation: The using namespace std; directive is used to bring the standard library namespace into scope.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the #include <iostream> directive in C++?

options.text: To include the iostream header file, which provides input/output functions
To exclude the iostream header file, which provides input/output functions
To declare a function, variable, or class
To define a function, variable, or class

mcqs.explanation: The #include <iostream> directive is used to include the iostream header file, which provides input/output functions.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the std::cout object in C++?

options.text: To output data to the console
To input data from the console
To declare a function, variable, or class
To define a function, variable, or class

mcqs.explanation: The std::cout object is used to output data to the console.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the std::cin object in C++?

options.text: To input data from the console
To output data to the console
To declare a function, variable, or class
To define a function, variable, or class

mcqs.explanation: The std::cin object is used to input data from the console.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the std::endl object in C++?

options.text: To insert a newline character and flush the output buffer
To insert a newline character without flushing the output buffer
To declare a function, variable, or class
To define a function, variable, or class

mcqs.explanation: The std::endl object is used to insert a newline character and flush the output buffer.
